name: Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail
name_original: Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes, El Pi Cargolat, Mirador del Corredor – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the moderate Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail, a 5.3-mile route through varied forests to an ancient dolmen and panoramic viewpoints.
region_intro: Hiking around Dosrius is primarily defined by its location within the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor, offering diverse landscapes and varied terrain. The region features extensive pine, holm oak, oak, and cork oak forests, creating a rich natural environment. Hilly terrain characterizes the area, with trails often following natural pathways through valleys and sometimes crossing streams. These physical features provide a mix of accessible paths and steeper climbs with elevation gains. Best hiking trails around Dosrius The most popular hiking route is Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to an ancient megalithic burial site within varied forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Corredor Sanctuary – Santuario del Corredor loop from Santuari del Corredor, an easy 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path. This trail offers views from the 16th-century sanctuary, a popular starting point for walks. Local hikers also love the Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes loop from Turó de Bell-lloc, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through diverse woodlands, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around Dosrius is defined by diverse forests, hilly terrain within the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park, and historical attractions like ancient dolmens. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Dosrius are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Dosrius's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Dosrius?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes around Dosrius, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include 29 easy trails, 30 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Dosrius?
A: Hiking in Dosrius offers diverse natural landscapes, primarily within the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor. You'll encounter extensive pine forests along coastal slopes, as well as holm oak, oak, and cork oak forests in the interior. The terrain is hilly, with trails often following natural pathways through valleys and sometimes crossing streams, providing varied scenery and panoramic views of the Maresme coast.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, Dosrius is rich in historical and archaeological sites. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950411422/dolmen-of-ca-l-arenes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt;, a Neolithic burial site. Another notable historical point is the 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6958805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Corredor&lt;/a&gt;, which offers excellent views. You can also find the curious 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6957759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyamars Ice Well&lt;/a&gt; hidden in the forest, or the prehistoric Sepulchral Cave of the Costa de Can Martorell.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Dosrius?
A: Yes, many routes in Dosrius are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6958805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary – Santuario del Corredor loop from Santuari del Corredor&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.5 km path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23819782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes loop from Turó de Bell-lloc&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.8 km trail through diverse woodlands. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19181457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarau Hill – Moreneta Spring loop from Dosrius&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 11.45 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Dosrius?
A: The hiking trails in Dosrius are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse forests, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest like the ancient dolmens and the Sanctuary of the Corredor.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Absolutely. Dosrius offers 29 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6958805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary – Santuario del Corredor loop from Santuari del Corredor&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.5 km path that provides beautiful views. Another family-friendly option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6957759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;E – Ice Well of Canyamars loop from Canyamars&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.66 km route that leads to a unique historical site.

Q: What are some interesting natural monuments or caves I can find nearby?
A: Beyond the main trails, you can explore several natural monuments and caves. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6227262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Swallows' Rock&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/887935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Foradada&lt;/a&gt;, both natural rock formations. For those interested in caves, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5785158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Frog&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3491408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de les Encantades&lt;/a&gt; offer intriguing explorations.

Q: Can I find places to rest or take shelter along the trails?
A: Yes, within the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park, there are several huts and shelters. You might come across places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5803300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabanya dels Caçadors&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4273231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugi de la Ferreria del Vedat&lt;/a&gt;. These can be useful for a break or in case of unexpected weather.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Dosrius?
A: Dosrius is suitable for hiking year-round due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Even in winter, the mild climate allows for comfortable hikes, especially through the diverse forests. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Is Dosrius easily accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: Dosrius is located within the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, the region is generally accessible. For detailed information on public transport options to the area, it's advisable to check local transport schedules or consider driving to a starting point with available parking.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or recommendations for hiking in the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park?
A: When hiking in the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor, it's always recommended to stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment. Respect local wildlife and vegetation, and carry out all your waste. For more detailed information about the park's regulations and facilities, you can visit the official park website or resources like &lt;a href="https://www.visitpineda.com/en/experiencia/montnegre/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitpineda.com&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Parc_del_Montnegre_i_el_Corredor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikivoyage&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
From this viewpoint we can see an important part of the Maresme mountains and part of the Barcelonès.

In the first place and in front of us we have the summit of Montalt and the Serra Polseruda which cover the municipalities of Sant Vicenç de Montalt and Sant Andreu de Llavaneres. We turn to the right and in the Litoral mountain range we can distinguish Montcabrer, Burriac castle and further to the right the antenna of the Céllecs hill.

In the background and in the distance, if the day is clear, you can see the Collserola mountain range where the communication tower and the expiatory temple of the Sacred Heart of Tibidabo stand out.
A megalithic tomb approximately 5,000 years old. Discovered in 1997 and restored in 2006 and 2007.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a moderate hike that generally takes around 2 to 3 hours to complete at a leisurely pace. While the komoot tour suggests approximately 2 hours and 30 minutes, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend exploring the points of interest.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, while rated as moderate, the trail is generally accessible for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. It's a circular route primarily following paths and tracks. Be prepared for some irregular terrain with stones and narrow sections, and always wear appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, predominantly following unpaved paths and tracks. You'll encounter sections with stones and some narrow passages, but these are typically manageable. The route winds through diverse vegetation, including pines, holm oaks, and cork oaks, characteristic of the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The highlight of the trail is undoubtedly the ancient Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes, a megalithic burial site. You'll also pass by the Corredor Viewpoint, offering panoramic views, and the unique Pi Cargolat (Twisted Stone Pine). Other notable features include the Font del Ferro (Iron Fountain) and the Masia Can Miloca farmhouse.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn being particularly good for mushroom spotting. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, along with plenty of water and sun protection. Winter hiking is also possible, but check weather conditions for potential colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmen of Ca l'Arenes Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor or to hike this specific trail. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife or vegetation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Dosrius, Catalunya. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, many hikers access the trail from areas within the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor. Look for designated parking spots near trailheads or visitor centers within the park, such as around the Can Bosc farmhouse, which is a starting point for some routes.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Catalunya E15, GR 92 BTT, and Canyamars - Santuari del Corredor trails. It's also part of the longer Montgat-Tordera and TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània routes for a portion of its length.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural park setting, the nearby area of Dosrius or other villages surrounding the Parc Natural del Montnegre i el Corredor may offer cafes, restaurants, and basic amenities. For a more inclusive experience, a shorter route adapted for individuals with mobility challenges starts from the Can Bosc farmhouse, which might have some basic facilities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a comfortable hike, we recommend bringing appropriate footwear (hiking shoes or boots), sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are advisable. A map or GPS device is also useful, though the trail is well-signposted as a local path (SL-C 80) with white and green markers.
